sections.title: The single thing they should remember
sections.text: The death-resurrection-salvation arc appears across cultures because it is real — the physics of coherence restoration projected by the Logos — not because cultures fabricate it from a shared template. Carrier's mythicist Bayesian argument fails on the reference class: treating pattern convergence as evidence of fabrication is a category error, like treating the universality of sunrise accounts as evidence the sun doesn't rise. The pattern is not noise. The pattern is signal.

sections.title: Critical Insight
sections.text:
Why it matters now
Correcting only Carrier's prior from 1/2 to 2/1 — keeping every other input he chose — flips his posterior from 32% to ~49%. Add the unique markers Jesus alone has in the class (independent attestation within one generation, hostile attestation, zero-power-base movement explosion, falsifiable claims unrefuted by living witnesses) and the corrected probability of historicity moves into the 49–97% range. The framework doesn't just defend Jesus's historicity — it predicts it.
If you've read this far in the series, you've seen something unusual. You've seen quantum mechanics and Genesis describing the same event. You've seen the Trinity derived from the Born Rule. You've seen the Fall as a phase transition, time as grace, the Cross as entropy reversal. And every piece of it was built on the assumption that Jesus of Nazareth was a real person who really lived, really died, and really rose.
— The Question
So what happens when someone says he wasn't?
The strongest version of that argument comes from Richard Carrier , a historian who applied Bayes' Theorem — the same probabilistic logic used in medical diagnosis, criminal forensics, and signal processing — to the question of whether Jesus existed. His conclusion: at best, a 32% chance Jesus was historical. Probably lower. His book On the Historicity of Jesus is peer-reviewed and methodologically serious. It is not a crank argument. It deserves a real answer.
This article gives one. Not by dismissing Carrier's method — Bayes' Theorem is correct, and using it on historical questions is legitimate. The problem isn't his math. It's his reference class. And the framework we've built across this series exposes exactly why.
I. How Carrier's Argument Works
The Bayesian equation is simple: posterior odds equal prior odds times the likelihood ratio. You start with how likely something is before you look at the evidence, then you update based on what the evidence says.
Carrier's Posterior
$$P_{\text{hist}} \;=\; P[1/2] \times X[576/1250] \times A[36/50] \times G[1/1] \times E^{\text{con}}[4/5 \cdot 3/5 \cdot 3/4] \times E^{\text{pro}}[2/1 \cdot 2/1 \cdot 2/1]$$
Result: 5,971,968 / 12,500,000 ≈ 32%.
Carrier's prior probability — his starting point before examining any evidence — comes from asking: in the set of all ancient figures most similar to Jesus, how often do they turn out to be historical rather than mythical? He places Jesus in the "heavily mythologized savior heroes" class: Osiris, Hercules, Romulus, Moses, Aesop. Figures who were worshiped, whose stories were filled with supernatural events, and who appeared primarily in sacred literature rather than mundane political records.
In that class, Carrier argues, at most 1 in 3 turned out to be historical. So he starts with a 33% prior — 1 to 2 odds that Jesus existed.
He then examines four categories of evidence: extra-biblical sources, the book of Acts, the Gospels, and the Epistles. Most of the evidence is equally likely whether Jesus existed or not (likelihood ratio of 1/1 — no effect). Some evidence slightly favors mythicism. Three ambiguous passages in Paul slightly favor historicity. The math shakes out to roughly 32% at the upper bound.

sections.title: The Pivot
sections.text:
The argument is clean. The math is correct given his inputs. The problem is upstream of the math.
II. The Reference Class Problem
Carrier's entire prior — and therefore his entire conclusion — depends on one assumption: that the structural similarities between Jesus and other savior-hero figures (incarnation, death, resurrection, salvation, worshiped by followers) are evidence of fabrication . He looks at the pattern and concludes: this is what cultures invent. Jesus shares the pattern. Therefore Jesus was probably invented.
This assumption does real work in his argument. It's the reason Jesus lands in the low-frequency reference class instead of a higher one. It's the reason the prior is 33% instead of, say, 75%. Everything downstream of that assumption inherits it.
But what if the assumption is wrong? What if the reason the death-resurrection-salvation pattern appears across multiple cultures isn't that humans keep inventing it — but that the pattern is real ?
III. The Projection Principle
This series has argued — with 10 property-by-property confirmations across physics and theology — that physical laws carry the structural signature of the being who made them. We call this the Projection Principle .
Rationality → Mathematics works
The substrate is intelligible because the source is rational.
Self-consistency → Universal laws
The same physics holds everywhere because the source is non-contradictory.
Conservation → Noether's theorem
Symmetry yields conservation; the Logos is symmetric in its commitments.
Relationality → Four forces are couplings
Reality is built of relations, not isolates — because the source is relational.
Triadic structure → Born Rule
Probability requires three irreducible operations: prepare, evolve, measure.
Coherence restoration → Death-and-return
When the entropy drain S·χ(t) is overcome by grace input G — that's the shape.
If the Projection Principle is true, then the death-resurrection-salvation arc is not a mythological template that cultures copy from each other. It is the physics of coherence restoration — what it looks like when the entropy drain is overcome by a grace input. It is the shape of reality reasserting itself after a symmetry-breaking event. It shows up in Osiris and Hercules and Romulus and the Eleusinian mysteries for the same reason the conservation of energy shows up in every culture that does careful measurements: because it's there .
The pattern is not noise. The pattern is signal.
IV. What This Does to Carrier's Math
If the pattern is signal rather than noise, the frequency of that pattern appearing in mythology does not reduce the prior probability of any individual instance being historical. To the contrary — it raises the question every signal-processing engineer asks: given that the signal is real, which instance is the source?
Carrier's reference class — "heavily mythologized savior heroes" — treats every member of the class as equally likely to be fabricated because they all share the pattern. But if the pattern exists because reality actually has a death-resurrection-salvation architecture, then the class isn't "people who were fabricated using this template." The class is "people onto whom a real signal was projected." Those are different classes with different base rates.
Carrier's Class
"People who were fabricated using this template." Pattern frequency → lower prior.

sections.title: Corrected Class
sections.text:
"People onto whom a real signal was projected." Pattern frequency → identifies the signal ; doesn't dilute any specific instance.
— The Sunrise Analogy
Suppose you find that 50 different ancient cultures described a bright light that appears in the east every morning. A Carrier-style argument would say: "The 'sunrise' pattern appears too frequently across too many mythologized accounts — clearly a fabricated motif." The problem is obvious. The sunrise pattern appears everywhere because the sun actually rises.
The death-resurrection-salvation arc appears across cultures because the Logos projects it. Every culture that listened closely enough picked up the signal. Some garbled it. Some mythologized it. Some got remarkably close. But the signal was always real. The question isn't "what percentage of these accounts are fabricated?" The question is: which account, if any, is the source event — the actual sunrise, not just the report of one?
V. Which Instance Is the Source?
If we grant that the pattern is real — that reality actually contains a coherence-restoration mechanism — then we need different criteria for identifying the source instance. Not "does this figure look mythologized?" (they all will). Instead: "which instance has the markers of an actual historical event rather than a projection?"
Those markers exist. Jesus is the only figure in the class who has all of them.
Marker 1 · Independent attestation within one generation
Paul's letters date to the 50s AD — roughly 20 years after the crucifixion. Paul claims to have met people who knew Jesus personally ( Gal 1:18–19 ). No other figure in Carrier's reference class has anything comparable. Osiris has no independent attestation within a millennium. Hercules has none within several centuries. Romulus has none within five hundred years.
Marker 2 · Hostile attestation
Tacitus (c. 116 AD) mentions Christ's execution under Pontius Pilate — and calls Christianity a "pernicious superstition." No motive to affirm. Josephus (c. 93 AD) references Jesus in a passage whose core is accepted as authentic by the majority of scholars even after removing later Christian interpolations. No other figure in the class has hostile attestation.
Marker 3 · Zero-power-base movement explosion
The marker Carrier underweights most severely . Every other figure in his class was either adopted by existing power structures or served the interests of existing institutions. Osiris was the state religion of Egypt. Romulus was the founding myth of Rome's ruling class. Hercules was woven into imperial propaganda. Jesus's movement began among fishermen and tax collectors in an occupied territory, was actively persecuted by both Jewish authorities and the Roman state, and nonetheless became the dominant religion of the empire within three centuries.
Marker 4 · Falsifiable claims his contemporaries didn't refute
Paul, writing in the 50s AD to communities that included people alive during Jesus's ministry, makes claims about Jesus's death and resurrection that would have been trivially easy to refute if false. The tomb was in Jerusalem. The crucifixion was public. The claimed witnesses were named and alive ( 1 Cor 15:6 — "most of whom are still alive"). These are not the kinds of claims a fabricator makes when the alleged witnesses are still walking around.
Each marker, individually, shifts the likelihood ratio in favor of historicity. Collectively, they are devastating to Carrier's conclusion — because his math depends on the evidence being weak. With a corrected reference class and properly weighted markers, the posterior probability of Jesus's historicity moves well above 50%.
VI. The Framework's Additional Evidence
This series adds something standard historical arguments don't have: a mechanism .
Carrier dismisses the resurrection as "supernatural" and assigns it a vanishingly small prior. Standard historiography agrees: miracles can't be established by historical method. But the framework doesn't treat the resurrection as a miracle in the traditional sense. It treats it as a predicted physical event — a local entropy reversal driven by maximum coherence-field coupling.

$$\dfrac{dC}{dt}\bigg|_{\text{event}} = G - S\!\cdot\!\chi(t) \;\;\to\;\; G \gg S\!\cdot\!\chi(t)$$

sections.title: Category Correction
sections.text:
Carrier can't assign a vanishingly small prior to an event for which there is both a theoretical mechanism and experimental evidence of the underlying coupling. His treatment of the resurrection as "supernatural" is a category error. The framework reclassifies it as a predicted coherence-restoration event — and the experimental data supports the reclassification.
VII. Running the Corrected Math
Three corrections are warranted. Each follows directly from the Projection Principle, not from special pleading.

sections.title: Correction 1
sections.text:
The prior  ·  1/2 → 2/1
If the Projection Principle is correct, the convergence of the pattern across figures is evidence the pattern is real, not evidence of fabrication. The correct reference class — given independent attestation, hostile attestation, and a movement that exploded from zero power base — is "historically attested religious founders with early independent sources." Base rate: at least 2 in 3.

sections.title: Correction 2
sections.text:
Extra-biblical evidence  ·  576/1250 → 1/1
Carrier's scoring depends on the assumption that the absence of stronger evidence is itself evidence for mythicism. The framework argues the opposite: the evidence is exactly what we expect for a non-elite figure in an occupied territory whose movement was persecuted by the very institutions that preserved records. Corrected to 1/1 — no effect either direction. (More conservative than the hostile attestations warrant.)

sections.title: Correction 3
sections.text:
Epistolary silences  ·  4/5 · 3/5 · 3/4 → 4/5
Carrier's counter-evidence consists of Paul's "silences" — things Paul doesn't mention about Jesus. The framework provides a reason: if what Paul experienced was the risen Christ through Logos-field coupling (which the PEAR-LAB data confirms is real), then Paul's emphasis on revelation over biography is exactly what we expect. The silences aren't suspicious. They're predicted.

sections.title: Corrected Posterior
sections.text:
$$P_{\text{hist}} \;=\; P[2/1] \times X[1/1] \times A[36/50] \times G[1/1] \times E^{\text{con}}[4/5] \times E^{\text{pro}}[8/1]$$
$$\;=\; \dfrac{2 \cdot 1 \cdot \tfrac{36}{50} \cdot 1 \cdot \tfrac{4}{5} \cdot 8}{1} \;=\; \dfrac{36.864}{1}$$
Posterior: 36.864 / 37.864 ≈ 97.4%

sections.title: All three corrections
sections.text:
Even keeping Carrier's Acts scoring and his own generous-to-historicity weighting, correcting only the prior from 1/2 to 2/1 flips his calculation: posterior ≈ 48.9%, nearly equipoise. That's the minimal correction. Add a modest fix to the extra-biblical scoring and the posterior jumps to ~66%. Add the framework's mechanism for Paul's silences and it goes above 90%.
— The Lever
The entire edifice rests on the reference class. Correct the reference class — and the math corrects itself.
VIII. What We Are Not Claiming
This article does not claim to have proven Jesus existed with mathematical certainty. Bayes' Theorem produces probabilities, not proofs. The claim is narrower and more precise:
Carrier's reference class is contaminated by a real signal.
The death-resurrection-salvation pattern appears across cultures because it is projected by the Logos into every domain — not because cultures fabricate it from a shared template.
Treating the pattern as evidence of fabrication is a category error — like treating the universality of sunrise accounts as evidence the sun doesn't rise.
With a corrected reference class, even Carrier's own math produces a probability of historicity well above 50%.
The corrections are not ad hoc . They follow from the Projection Principle, which this series has argued for across 10 articles with independent evidence from quantum mechanics, thermodynamics, information theory, and experimental consciousness research.
The framework doesn't just defend Jesus's historicity — it predicts it. If the Logos is real, and the coherence-restoration arc is real, then the source event must have happened. Not because theology requires it, but because the physics requires a source for the signal.
And Jesus is the only candidate in the historical record with the evidentiary markers of a source event.
The pattern is not noise. The pattern is signal. And the signal has a name.
IX. Falsification Criteria
A theory worth holding is a theory that can be killed. Five conditions would break this argument:
Kill 1 · Projection Principle fails
If the 10 property-by-property mappings between Logos properties and physical laws can be shown to be cherry-picked or post-hoc rather than predictive, the reference class correction loses its foundation.
Kill 2 · Pattern is purely cultural transmission
If the death-resurrection-salvation pattern can be shown to have a purely cultural transmission mechanism (direct borrowing, no independent origination), the "signal not noise" argument weakens.
Kill 3 · PEAR-LAB / GCP falsified
If the experimental support for consciousness coupling is definitively falsified, the framework loses its mechanism for the resurrection.
Kill 4 · Another figure has the markers
If another figure in the reference class is found with comparable markers (independent + hostile attestation, zero-power-base explosion), Jesus's uniqueness as the source event is no longer assured.
Kill 5 · Reference class defended without fabrication assumption
If Carrier's class can be defended on grounds that don't assume the pattern is fabricated, the correction doesn't apply.

What we got right, what we're less sure about, and where we got carried away.

sections.title: Reference class critique
sections.text: Carrier's entire prior depends on treating pattern convergence as evidence of fabrication. If the pattern is real — which the Projection Principle argues with 10/10 confirmations — then the reference class is contaminated by signal. This is not a theological objection. It's a statistical one: you cannot use the frequency of a real phenomenon to argue against the reality of any specific instance of it.

sections.title: Unique evidentiary markers
sections.text: Independent attestation within one generation, hostile attestation, zero-power-base movement explosion — no other figure in Carrier's class has all three. Not disputed even by mythicist scholars; they simply weight these markers differently.

sections.title: The math is correct
sections.text:
Correcting only the prior from 1/2 to 2/1 and keeping everything else Carrier argues produces a posterior near 49%. This is arithmetic, not interpretation.

sections.title: Projection Principle is itself the load-bearing assumption
sections.text:
If it falls, the reference class correction falls with it. The 10/10 mapping is strong but has not been independently verified outside the framework. A skeptic will note the circularity — we're using our conclusion to fix someone else's premise. The response: the Projection Principle makes independent predictions (like the PEAR-LAB result) confirmed by external data. The circularity concern is legitimate and should be stated.
"Movement explosion" is causally ambiguous
Christianity's growth from zero power base is unusual, but Carrier has responses: sociological reasons unrelated to the founder's existence (network effects, appeal to the marginalized, Roman infrastructure). The marker is evidence, not proof.

sections.title: Corrected prior of 2/1 is a judgment call
sections.text: The exact base rate in the corrected class is debatable. A more conservative correction might land at 1/1 (50/50) instead of 2/1. Even at 1/1, the posterior still improves significantly over Carrier's result.

sections.title: Where we got carried away
sections.text:
The 97.4% figure is aspirational, not demonstrated
It depends on all three corrections being accepted simultaneously, each at the values we chose. A more honest range: prior-only ~49%; prior + extra-biblical ~66%; all corrections ~97%. The range 49–97% is more defensible than the point estimate.
PEAR-LAB → resurrection mechanism is a reach
PEAR-LAB confirms consciousness-matter coupling exists. It does not confirm that a specific individual achieved maximum coupling and reversed entropy at a specific historical moment. The gap between "consciousness affects RNGs at small effect sizes" and "a man rose from the dead" is large. The framework provides the theoretical bridge, but the experimental confirmation is at a much lower magnitude than the event it's used to support.

sections.title: The Disclaimer
sections.text: We are finite minds reasoning about infinite God. Every model we construct is a projection of higher-dimensional reality onto a lower-dimensional surface we can comprehend. We do not claim to have captured God in equations. We claim that when we look at His creation honestly — with the tools of physics and the revelation of Scripture — the same structure appears in both. Where our model limits what God can be, the limitation is ours, not His. We offer this work as worship, not as containment.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.text:
When physics, theology, information theory, neuroscience, thermodynamics, and history all independently produce the same structural architecture — without anyone coordinating them — that convergence is either a coincidence or a signal. This article makes the case that it is signal.
The framework did not start with theology and look for physics to support it. It did not start with physics and force theology into the math. It started with the coherence equation and let the structure lead. At every stage, the mapping was tested against the strongest available objections. Where it broke, it was corrected. Where it held, it was extended. The Incarnation-as-symmetry-breaking insight was forced by the physics — not imposed by the theology.
Independent convergence across formally distinct domains is the strongest evidence type in science. When your prediction from one domain is confirmed by data from another domain that you never consulted, that is not cherry-picking. That is structural isomorphism producing cross-domain predictions — and when those predictions land, the architecture is telling you something.
The claim: The pattern IS the signal. Not a pattern that suggests a signal. Not a pattern consistent with a signal. The degree of cross-domain convergence documented across this series — with falsifiable predictions, control group confirmation, and formal mathematical structure — is either the most elaborate coincidence in intellectual history or evidence that physics and theology are projections of a single underlying architecture.
Rigor & Kill Conditions
Every claim is held to explicit falsification standards.

Without the Projection Principle, the reference-class correction loses its foundation and the entire "pattern is signal" argument collapses.

sections.title: Mathematics
sections.text:
Every equation that appears in the paper, collected here as a reference index. Equations remain in the paper body where they were introduced.
Bayes' Theorem (Odds Form)
$$\text{posterior odds} \;=\; \text{prior odds} \times \text{likelihood ratio}$$
The probability rule the article inherits from Carrier — the same logic used in medical diagnosis, criminal forensics, and signal processing.
Plain English: Start with how likely something is before evidence; multiply by how strongly the evidence cuts. Carrier's method is correct; the article's claim is that one of his inputs — the prior, derived from a contaminated reference class — is wrong.
Carrier's Posterior
$$P_{\text{hist}} = P\!\left[\tfrac{1}{2}\right] \times X\!\left[\tfrac{576}{1250}\right] \times A\!\left[\tfrac{36}{50}\right] \times G[1] \times E^{\text{con}}\!\left[\tfrac{4}{5}\!\cdot\!\tfrac{3}{5}\!\cdot\!\tfrac{3}{4}\right] \times E^{\text{pro}}\!\left[\tfrac{2}{1}\!\cdot\!\tfrac{2}{1}\!\cdot\!\tfrac{2}{1}\right]$$
$$\approx \tfrac{5{,}971{,}968}{12{,}500{,}000} \approx 32\%$$
P = prior. X = extra-biblical sources. A = Acts. G = Gospels. E con = three Pauline silences. E pro = three Pauline historicity passages.
Plain English: Carrier's upper-bound result. The math is clean given his inputs; the article's whole correction operates upstream of the math — on the reference class that fixes the prior.
Corrected Posterior (All Three Fixes)
$$P_{\text{hist}} = P\!\left[\tfrac{2}{1}\right] \times X[1] \times A\!\left[\tfrac{36}{50}\right] \times G[1] \times E^{\text{con}}\!\left[\tfrac{4}{5}\right] \times E^{\text{pro}}\!\left[\tfrac{8}{1}\right] = \tfrac{36.864}{1}$$
$$\Rightarrow \tfrac{36.864}{37.864} \approx 97.4\%$$
Three corrections: (1) prior 1/2 → 2/1 (Projection-Principle reference class), (2) extra-biblical 576/1250 → 1/1 (no-effect, conservative), (3) epistolary silences collapsed because the framework predicts them as Logos-coupling revelation.
Plain English: Even keeping Carrier's Acts and Gospel scoring, the fully corrected posterior is ~97%. The lever is the reference class — correct it, and the math corrects itself.

Each marker, individually, shifts the likelihood ratio toward historicity. Collectively, they uniquely identify Jesus among Carrier's reference class.

Projection Principle — Property Mapping

Six instances cited in this article: rationality → mathematics works; self-consistency → universal laws; conservation → Noether's theorem; relationality → four forces as couplings; triadic structure → Born Rule; coherence restoration → death-and-return arc.

Reference Class Correction (the Lever)

Same mathematical machinery; different denominator.
Plain English: Sunrise analogy: 50 cultures describing a bright light in the east each morning is not evidence the sun was fabricated. Carrier's class assumes the signal is fake; correcting that assumption swaps the denominator and the math comes out the other way.

sections.title: Glossary
sections.text:
Key terms used in this article, defined as the article itself uses them.
Bayes' Theorem
A probability rule for updating belief in light of evidence. Posterior odds = prior odds × likelihood ratio. The article accepts the method and corrects the input.

sections.title: Posterior / Prior / Likelihood Ratio
sections.text:
Posterior = belief after evidence. Prior = belief before evidence. Likelihood ratio = how strongly evidence cuts. Carrier's prior is the variable the article reweights.
Carrier (Richard)
Historian who applied Bayes' Theorem to Jesus's historicity ( On the Historicity of Jesus ). Concluded ~32% probability of historicity. The article's interlocutor — treated as methodologically serious, not crank.

sections.title: Mythicism
sections.text: The position that Jesus was a mythological / literary construction rather than a historical person. Carrier's conclusion at the upper bound; the article's correction moves the posterior the other way.

sections.title: Reference Class
sections.text: The set of similar cases used to compute a base rate. The article's central claim: Carrier's class — “heavily mythologized savior heroes” — is contaminated by treating signal as noise.

sections.title: Reference Class Problem
sections.text: The well-known difficulty in applied probability that the answer depends on which set of comparables you place a case in. The article uses this canonical critique against Carrier's specific choice.

sections.title: Heavily Mythologized Savior Heroes
sections.text: Carrier's reference class. Includes Osiris, Hercules, Romulus, Moses, Aesop. The article argues these are projections of a real pattern, not the fabrication template Carrier treats them as.

sections.title: Projection Principle
sections.text: The article's foundation: physical laws carry the structural signature of the Logos that made them. Each property of the Logos shows up as an isomorphic property of the substrate. Six examples cited.

sections.title: Logos
sections.text:
In this article, the rational, self-consistent, conservative, relational, triadic source whose signature is detectable in physics. The being from whom the death-resurrection-salvation pattern is projected.
Logos Field ($\chi$)
The pervasive substrate of coherent reality — the framework's $\chi$ field; identified with the Master Equation's coherence field. Note: earlier drafts denoted this with $\Lambda$; that symbol is now reserved for the cosmological constant.

sections.title: Logos Coupling
sections.text:
The interaction architecture between the Logos field and the systems embedded in it — the channel structure ($C_0$, $C_1$, inter-substrate coupling) that determines how the field acts on a subsystem. Distinct from the field itself: field = pervasive substrate; coupling = how interactions occur within it.
Entropy / Sin ($S$)
The decay parameter that drives coherence toward zero. Same Master-Equation variable, two coordinate names: “entropy” in the physics frame, “sin” in the theological frame. Activated in the human domain at the Fall.

sections.title: Coherence Restoration / Death-Resurrection-Salvation Arc
sections.text:
The shape that appears across cultures because reality has a coherence-restoration mechanism. What it looks like when the entropy drain S·χ(t) is overcome by grace input G.
Source Event vs. Projection
The article's distinction. Many cultural accounts are projections of a real pattern; one is the source event the projections reflect. The four markers are how you tell them apart.

sections.title: Sunrise Analogy
sections.text: The article's central image. 50 cultures describing a bright light in the east is not evidence of a shared fabrication; it is evidence the sun rises. Pattern frequency identifies signal, doesn't dilute any specific instance.

sections.title: Category Error
sections.text:
The article's diagnosis of Carrier's prior — treating pattern convergence as evidence of fabrication. Same shape error as treating universal sunrise accounts as evidence the sun doesn't rise.
Independent Attestation (Marker 1)
Paul's letters (50s AD) attest Jesus within ~20 years of the crucifixion and reference people who knew him personally (Galatians 1:18–19). No other figure in Carrier's class has anything within a millennium for Osiris, several centuries for Hercules, or 500+ years for Romulus.
Hostile Attestation (Marker 2)
Tacitus (~116 AD) calls Christianity “a pernicious superstition” while affirming Christ's execution under Pilate. Josephus (~93 AD) references Jesus in a passage whose core is mainstream-accepted as authentic. No motive to confirm; confirmation present anyway.
Zero-Power-Base Movement Explosion (Marker 3)
The marker the article says Carrier underweights most. Other figures in the class were adopted by existing power structures; the Jesus movement began among fishermen and tax collectors, was actively persecuted, and became dominant within three centuries.
Unrefuted Falsifiables (Marker 4)
Paul (1 Corinthians 15:6) names ~500 living witnesses to the resurrection while writing to communities that included people alive during Jesus's ministry. Trivially refutable by contemporaries if false; not refuted.

sections.title: Epistolary Silences
sections.text:
Carrier's E con evidence: things Paul doesn't mention about Jesus. The article's framework explanation: if Paul's experience was direct Logos-field coupling to the risen Christ, his emphasis on revelation over biography is predicted, not suspicious.
Coherence (C)
Alignment with the Logos source. In this article, the variable that reaches its theoretical maximum at the resurrection event when G overwhelms the entropy drain.
Grace (G)
The negentropic input term in the coherence equation. At the resurrection, G ≫ S·χ(t) — coherence reaches its maximum.
Entropy Drain (S·χ(t))
The decay term acting on the χ field. The thing the resurrection event overcomes.

sections.title: Maximum-Coherence Event
sections.text: The article's name for the resurrection in framework terms: a predicted physical event in which a fully Logos-coupled consciousness drives C to its theoretical maximum, overcoming the local entropy drain.

sections.title: Cross-Frame Operation
sections.text: The Cross operates simultaneously in both physics frames: at the GR level, a man dies at definite spacetime coordinates; at the quantum level, “the Lamb slain from the foundation of the world” operates outside temporal ordering.

sections.title: PEAR-LAB
sections.text:
Princeton Engineering Anomalies Research; ~6.35σ over ~2.5 million trials. The article's first empirical anchor for consciousness-coherence coupling. (Defined in detail in Article 16.)
Global Consciousness Project (GCP)
Roger Nelson's distributed-REG network; ~6σ cumulative over 325+ pre-registered events. The article's second empirical anchor.
Born Rule (Triadic Structure)
The probability rule of QM: P(outcome) = |⟨φ|ψ⟩| 2 . Cited as a Projection-Principle instance — three irreducible operations (prepare, evolve, measure / Father, Son, Spirit). (Defined in detail in Article 06.)
Noether's Theorem
Symmetry yields conservation. Cited as a Projection-Principle instance: the Logos's symmetric commitments project as conservation laws.

sections.title: Tacitus / Josephus
sections.text: Two early non-Christian sources. Tacitus (~116 AD): Christ executed under Pilate; Christianity called a “pernicious superstition.” Josephus (~93 AD): authentic core reference to Jesus accepted after later Christian interpolations are removed.

sections.title: Osiris / Hercules / Romulus
sections.text: The article's contrast cases. Each lacks one or more of the four source-event markers and was adopted by ruling-class religion or imperial propaganda — the structural opposite of the early Jesus movement.

sections.title: Falsifiable Claims
sections.text:
Claims that contemporary witnesses could trivially refute if false. Paul's resurrection claims, made while named witnesses were still alive, are read as a historicity marker rather than as faith propaganda.
"Lamb Slain from the Foundation of the World"
Revelation 13:8. Read as the eternal-frame view of the Cross — the resurrection reachable retrocausally from the foundation of the world. (Developed in detail in Article 18.)

sections.title: The Lever
sections.text: The article's term for what carries the whole edifice: the reference class. Correct it — from “fabrications” to “projections of a real signal” — and Carrier's own machinery flips the conclusion.

sections.title: Master Equation
sections.text: The framework's central expression: χ = ∫∫∫(G·M·E·S·T·K·R·Q·F·C) dx dy dt. The resurrection-as-maximum-coherence-event sits inside this equation.
